reviews: Finally tried Hammee’s!! It’s good - juicy beef patty with a nice char on the edges, fluffy buttery toasted potato buns, and sweet soft caramelised onions. I wished that it came with pickles by default, I’ll do an add on next time as I felt for the refreshing crunch + sour taste. Satisfied, and even more so at this price point. The triple cooked fries were also quite nice, was crisp and crunchy. Comparing it to OFC, I still prefer OFC but Hammee’s burgers are a very good, close alternative too. But OFC’s fries are better, those come with skins on!

Hammee’s Classic Double Cheeseburger features toasted burger buns, handmade 100% beef patty, caramelised onions, American cheese, chopped jalapenos and house made sauce Location: Hammee's Burgers, 31 Commonwealth Cres, #02-93 Market & Food Centre, Singapore 149644

One of the juiciest burgers I have tasted, buns have a fluffy core and a nice toasty crust. Caramelized onions that made it taste even better. $11.50 for a double cheeseburger; don't let its size fool you. The flavour and the satisfaction (filling up stomach space and tastebuds) is worth the money. I had it on an empty stomach and it was after am intense workout session. Filled me up.

Hmm I don't get the hype and wait time. This was only marginally better than hawker burgers. Maybe McDonald's standard. The patty, bun, cheese and pickle were so ordinary. There was caramelised onion but very little. The best component was the tater tots.

Finally able to try @hammees_sg, the famous burger at Commonwealth Crescent Market and food centre. It’s really good, worth my trip to go down. Feature the Classic beef cheese burger double patty ($11) and Hand Batter Fish burger double patty ($9.5). The beef cheese burger, the soft bun served with juicy tender beef patty, slow caramelised onion and homemade sauce. The hand batter fish burger, the soft bun served with fish patty( very crisp outside while inside juicy), cheese, homemade sauce. 📍Hammee’s. Commonwealth crescent market and food centre #02-93.
Decent burger, small serving of fries. Good price point. Not bad but don’t think it’s worth queuing too long for!

contact_and_links.phone: This place does not have a landline.
contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-93 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: rn_1604
reviews.text:
Bun is nice and crispy but meat patty is slightly disappointing as it is on the dryer side. Not sure if it’s because of the hype that I’m over expecting too much.

2023 update: beef burger is better now, juicer than before. Fries are nicely cooked, crispy on the outside and potatoey on the inside. Onion rings taste exactly like BK one.

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text:
Came at about 4pm and ordered a classic beef cheeseburger. It took only 10 mins for the buzzer to ring.
I started off with the fries and was amazed by it! It was very thick, the exterior was crispy and the inside was soft and dense. The cheeseburger was good too! Bun was fluffy and soft, beef patty was really juicy and doesn’t feel rubbery. Other than the cheese, I also saw some pickles and onions which give a nice crunchy texture when I eat. Yum!
Really enjoyed the cheeseburger and fries, and definitely will be back to try other burgers!

reviews.author: Timothy TM
reviews.text: The chicken burger was sold out so had the double cheeseburger and added bacon, fries and chicken cutlets. All for $19.50. The double cheeseburger is $12.50 and it’s really good! the patty was tasty and juicy, could do with more cheese but still v nice, bun is not dry. The fries (although serving is not big) and chicken were all really good too, these guys really know how to make a great tasting meal! They should expand to more places!

reviews.author: Jade Lee
reviews.text: Came here on a weekday. Ordered double cheese burger at 1.30pm and waited for 1 hour. The fries are yummies, different from other western eateries. The burger is so juicy and fragrance with onion topping. Believe the beef is of certain premium quality. If only you have time to wait, good to try at least once!!

reviews.author: S Wun
reviews.text:
Visit #1: I will adjust star rating after I tried the burgers. Putting this review as I visited the shop but it had this notice saying that closed temporarily until further notice. So just a shout out to those who are intending to go to commonwealth market to check this out.

Visit #2: finally managed to try Hammee’s and not after a wait for more than an hour. So many people ordering takeaways and delivery. Perhaps had overly high expectations. Was a tad disappointed that the beef patty was not as good as expected. But generally Hammee’s beef burger was juicy and delicious. Much better than similar burger hawker concept stalls I have tried. Also tried the chicken burger and that one was also good and not fatty. Meat was firm and not oily. The side of fries were very good and I would potentially order just the fries next time! Oh and I wanna try the fish burger one day.

reviews: Consist of MFJ Mix Platter Set* consist of: 1. Platter - 3 types of meat - 3 types of seafood - 1 type of poultry Meat: Ribeye,LambFlap,LambChop seafood: Prawn,Calamari & Mussels Poultry: Grill/Fried Chicken 2. Sides - 3 types; Wraps or Naan , Mix Salad and Cajun CutFries This is our standard mixed platter set.To book or enquire do whatsapp 90990809. There is a change of menu & operating hrs during Ramadan,Fasting month.

The halal Asian BBQ and Grill stall at the Food Junction is owned by a former contestant of Suria's King Juara Memasak 2014. I went with two of my good colleagues. After all, it was just a few minutes away from MOE Heritage Centre near our school. We were tired from having a long day of classes and we're hoping the journey would be worth it. It was! We didn't get lost this time even though we didn't use Google maps (much). It was a food junction ala hawker style. Thus, it was not air-conditioned and most stalls were self-service. The first thing we did was to order drinks. I had the chicken with beetroot (I forgot what it's called) sandwich which was tasty for something with beetroot in it. Honestly I was just trying to be adventurous and deviate from what I would usually order... turned out it appeared as if I was eating clean that day. My friends ordered some cheesy fries (omg it was so good, but anything with cheese IS good), and various steak and chicken chops. They gave a good verdict for their food too. I will be back to try more of their menu!
result.stall_name: MELA Food Junction (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-103 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Harriot NN
reviews.text:
Quiet food center with many stalls manned by locals. Recommend these 2 stalls:

1) Fried carrot cake - carrot cakes appear to be made in house, saw trays of steamed carrot cakes. Well fried with loads of chay poh.

2) 满天香 Dumplings - Steamed dumplings and fried beancurb rolls come with generous fillings and are so delicious.

reviews.author: Moonlight Beauty
reviews.text:
🍜 Pork Ball Noodle Soup – Light, Healthy & Comforting!

There’s a small stall near my house that serves Pork Ball Noodle Soup, and ever since it opened, I’ve been going there quite often 😋

The soup is clear and naturally sweet, not oily at all.
The pork balls mixed with black fungus are chewy and flavorful — not greasy, so it’s perfect for anyone who prefers healthy, light meals.
The noodles are soft, with fresh bean sprouts and spring onions, making the bowl look and taste so clean and refreshing 🌿

It’s one of those dishes that’s simple yet satisfying, perfect for people who want to eat light but still get enough nutrition.
Pair it with an iced coffee and it’s the perfect lunch combo in Singapore ☕️✨

👉 If you live nearby, you should really try this place — the soup’s gentle flavor will surprise you in the best way!

reviews.author: Jude Lu
reviews.text: You kind find some gems here but don't expect it to blow your mind. 2 chefs is what people usually come here for. The food center is ok. The area is stuck in time and the has a really nostalgic vibe. Good for a short exploration and a quick fuss free meal.

reviews.author: Michi
reviews.text:
Tucked away in an unassuming corner of the hawker centre, I stumbled upon what might just be one of the best bowls of minced pork porridge I’ve had in a long time. It wasn’t planned, nor was I expecting much—just a quick meal to tide me through the day. But oh, was I in for a surprise.

Surprisingly one of the best minced pork porridges I’ve had! The uncle was super generous with the meat, and the porridge was smooth and comforting. Added an egg, total came to $5.50—not the cheapest, but absolutely worth it. I can’t stop thinking about it!

The hawker centre is also clean and dry. The cleaners are also very polite and friendly!

reviews.author: Grace T
reviews.text: This hawker center has great vibes on Saturdays. Uncles will be singing oldies while aunties at stalls close by will be cheering for them. What an amazing feeling of just people coming together in harmony. Of course some party pooper got to complain about them and the uncles were asked to stop singing. If they didn’t like the singing, they could sit on the other side of the hawker center because it’s not possible to hear them from the other side.  But no, this person chose to take away the happiness of many others. Now it’s just another hawker center. For food, charcoal bbq meat, bak Chor mee and chicken rice are the best!

reviews: Henry's is a pretty popular chicken rice stall here at commonwealth, and with their the sheer overpowering % of roasted chicken hanging on display, u just know that the roasted chicken is more popular than the white steamed chicken. The roasted chicken here is rather good. Meat was tender and juicy. The rice was flavourful and fragrant. The chilli was rather Solid too.

Henry's Chicken Rice is where I've been eating since young. Even as the price increase and the portion has slightly decrease, the quality is still there. The rice is cooked with chicken stock and other ingredients, making it really fragrant. The braised chicken feet is well flavored with the spices and dark sauce; and the star of this place is the chili sauce which you would have to pay extra is you want more for takeaway.
This chicken rice stall has good roasted chicken. Not too roasted and the rice is good too.
Evenly roasted chicken drumstick and wing. This is one of the best roast chicken I've tasted.
It is so good and only $2.5!
result.stall_name: Henry's Chicken Rice (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.phone: This place does not have a land line
contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-84 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Jeannie Lau
reviews.text:
If you love comfort food, this chicken rice is a must-try. The chicken—both steamed and roasted—is tender, moist, and flavorful, well-marinated and perfectly paired with incredibly fragrant, aromatic, and fluffy rice. The balance of flavors is just right, and everything comes together so beautifully that it feels both simple and special.

The chilli sauce blended with ginger, vinegar and garlic is absolutely delicious, making all the difference as it perfectly complements the chicken rice.

It’s the kind of dish that’s comforting, satisfying, and truly memorable all at once.

Hands down one of the best chicken rice dishes I’ve had—super flavorful, comforting, and absolutely delicious. Highly recommended!

NOTE : closed on Wednesday and Thursday.

Available from 10am onwards till stock last (usually sold out by 1pm plus or earlier).

reviews.author: Nydxs
reviews.text: Don’t let the looks fool you. The roasted chicken’s actually quite tender, as opposed to belief that roasted chicken meat is usually harder in texture. Rice is full of flavour, and the soup has a slight onion taste due to the shallots in the soup, which don’t get me wrong, it’s good. I enjoy this experience and would come here again

reviews.author: Solitary Restless
reviews.text:
Best-ever chicken rice. The roast chicken is tender and flavorful, the rice is just nice, and the chili adds an amazing, sour kick to the meal.

Be prepared to wait, though. I arrived at 10 plus, and there was already a line of people ahead, and now, worse.

reviews.author: Peter Lin
reviews.text:
Roast Chicken Rice with Braised Chicken Feet. The roast chicken is probably one of the best I have eaten and its really quite difficult to find a good one.  They also have the non traditional braised chicken feet instead of the typical chicken glass feet.

June 2023

White Chicken and Roast Chicken + Braised Chicken Feet. This is probably the best roast chicken rice stall and the owners have been serving it since the 80's.  Their roasted chicken retains the tender and juicy chicken flesh. The chef learned his chicken rice skills from the famous atas Chatterbox chicken rice at Hilton Orchard.

reviews.author: Ronald Lam
reviews.text: Henry's Chicken Rice in Commonwealth Crescent Market has been consistently good. The succulent and flavorful chicken, expertly prepared, was the highlight of the meal. Each bite was tender and juicy, with a delightful blend of seasoning. The rice, cooked to perfection, was fragrant and complemented the chicken beautifully. The chili sauce had just the right amount of heat to add an extra layer of flavor. A must-visit for anyone seeking authentic and delicious chicken rice.

dishes: Lean Meat with Century Egg Porridge
reviews: Commonwealth Crescent has plenty of good food. And another solid stall here, is Hong Kee porridge. This is also another popular stall here, with decent Google ratings. This is the thick porridge style, not the watery teochew style.

Nostalgic and comforting bowl of Porridge filled with generous ingredient. So shiok. Location: Hong Kee Porridge, Commonwealth Crescent Market and Food Centre, 31 Commonwealth Cres, #02-89, Singapore 149644
$3 for the traditional tasty pork porridge that has slice pork and liver. Value for money.
Slow cooked, each type of meat and liver was added in on different timing. First spoonful recall childhood's memory, the taste so yummy. After taste was slightly sweet from the liver. Amazing for $3.
This is really old school porridge not too thick nor watery. It's a comfort food for me.

However, their sliced fish wasn't good. Not seasoned and tasted old. For those who are the true fan of fish porridge, please give their fish head + fish belly porridge a try. No regret. Hong Kee Porridge Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre, 31 Commonwealth Crescent, #02-89, Commonwealth, Singapore 149644 Daily from 0700H to 1400H
result.stall_name: Hong Kee Porridge (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-89 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: OB1
reviews.text:
One of the best porridge stalls in Commonwealth! Run by a nice elderly couple.  Despite reviews saying the uncle is fierce, they were actually very polite when I visited.

I had the mixed pork porridge with added century egg, egg, and sotong ($7.50). The porridge is the "watery" type and not too salty. Huge plus point: Uncle gives a lot of pork liver!

Will definitely return. Remember to bring CASH as they don't accept digital payments/PayLah.

reviews.author: YS Cheng
reviews.text: Really authentic Cantonese porridge stall. Is very light so good for breakfast or lunch. Anyway the stall will be closed by dinner time. Lots of ingredients and I added fish to my pork porridge. Cost $10 per bowl but worth it. The liver and fish were served rather raw so you’ve to wait awhile for them to be cooked by the hot porridge. Ingredients were fresh and blended very well with the porridge. Will definitely return to try out their fish porridge.

reviews.author: Nelson Lim
reviews.text:
Wah… Not many places I dare to say that the porridge very umami and never put MSG. Old school Cantonese porridge, with very home cooked feel. The same feel when you grandma cooks for you when you’re young.

They gave a whole century egg, liver is medium rare, meat is tender and the porridge is runny. Chilli is not the spicy type and adds to the flavour. Remember to ask for an egg to go with it.

Owner is getting old. Better eat this more before he retires. Not many of these places left. I really call this a hidden Jem.

reviews.author: Lawrence Ng
reviews.text:
Absolutely terrible. Saw an article about how this was an old school porriage place so we had to try. Joined the queue to order and the lady taking orders snarled at me asking what I wanted. That's cool maybe it's just her way of communicating, so I ordered 2 bowls of pork porriage without liver and without egg. She snapped at me that she will call out when it was done in 10 min. I came back 10 min later to witness her scolding a customer for not having a table number. The husband I assume the cook joined in to grumble "dunno how to come with table number don't eat!!!" in Cantonese. Was taken aback how rude they were. Then the lady who picked up her porriage order before me said her order was wrong and she wanted it without liver. She got a loud scolding from the porriage lady that they don't customise and just cut her off. Wow!!
When it came to my turn the porriage looked OK so went back to the table. Only to find it was LOADED with liver and blood oozing into the porriage. I knew there was no recourse having witnessed the previous exchanges so we just had to leave to porridge aside and vote with out wallets never to return and to write this warning review.

reviews.author: Shaolin Leong
reviews.text:
Cantonese porridge done to perfection !
Next texture & smooth. For those who appreciate century egg you can add ons.
Their fish head porridge is good if you know how to appreciate. Pork porridge is fantastic too !
This stall is managed by 2 old folks. Have been here for ages. Be clear in your instructions when ordering.
Uncle is almost 80 years old and fit as a bull. All their customers are loyal supporters .
Be clear in your order please !
Communication is 2 ways ! Can't help you if you don't even know what you ordered or how to order ....during busy lunch hours when the queue is long !!!

reviews: Black Carrot Cake (S$3) It’s actually quite soft and moist, sweet but not too sweet and plenty of charred bits to make it a great breakfast. Location: Liang Liang Fried Carrot Cake, Commonwealth Crescent Market and Food Centre, 31 Commonwealth Cres, #02-69, Singapore 149644

One of the best white carrot cake in Singapore. The uncle really cooks it until very thin and very crispy. At only $2.50, it is a big plate. He does both white and black version and they are both good.

Carrot Cake (Black/White) - White: slightly crisp and has lots of egg. Black: pieces of carrot cake well coated with sweet sauce and definitely better than white! Overall a very comforting plate of carrot cake!
I love black carrot cake and this version is soft and sweet. A plate costs only $2.50. It's really value for money. White carrot cake is also available.
result.stall_name: Liang Liang Fried Carrot Cake (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.phone: This place does not have a landline.
contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-69 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Mel
reviews.text:
Hawker food rarely gets good reviews unless they’re a modern twist to local food or overhyped by tourists so I’m hoping to add some colour by reviewing good old hawker haunts!

My friends enjoyed their plate of white ctk but I’m a black ctk girl at heart. Uncle was generous with eggs and chye poh and the price was very reasonable starting from 3.50$. There was a steady stream of customers the two hours I was there, but the queue moved quickly.

Would highly recommend washing it down with a cup of sugarcane juice!

reviews.author: Terence Lee
reviews.text:
Really nice fried carrot cake.
Texture is good, well fried and not too oily or dry. Generous amount of eggs and chai poh used.
Chilli spicy level just nice.
Reasonable pricing.
Friendly stall owner.
Definitely worth eating.

reviews.author: Holly K
reviews.text: One of my favourite places to get carrot cake! The carrot cake is soft and moist, seasoned perfectly with the right amount of salt and heat. Loveee the slightly charred pieces, it's so tasty! Both white and black carrot cakes are good. White is a must-try! (smallest size portion in photos)

reviews.author: g s
reviews.text: The black carrot cake is a little sweet but cooked until it has a good chew, which I don't  mind but perhaps it would be better a little softer.

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text: Went on a Sunday morning and the queue was pretty long. But uncle was frying very quickly with 2 hands! Ordered the white one because I know their white carrot cake is not the crispy type. But the soft type. I like it!! Ordered the $4 and was given a lot of eggs in it.

dishes: 本地美食-魚头米粉Local Favorite - Fish Head Noodle
reviews: Taste are at salty side, may be too much salty fish added.

This is as original as it gets. Each piece of the chicken wing is well marinated and coated with tasty prawn paste. The staff here averaged 50/60 years old and is very experienced in traditional cooking. Yummy. Posted by Ah Leong San 2015 August 17.
Simple dish! So so only !
由猛火滾燙生魚頭而成的湯鮮甜濃洧，讚 !
result.stall_name: Hong Kong Street Zhen Ji (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.phone: This place does not have a landline.
contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-102 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

google.google_name: HK Street Chun Kee 香港街珍记

reviews.author: T Tan
reviews.text:
Maybe not outstanding.. But definitely tasty for sure and prices / service / hygiene are excellent.

Corkage $10.

reviews.author: Mee Hiong Yap
reviews.text: The food is so good! Highly recommended their Ha Jeong Chicken! Crispy and well marinated.

reviews.author: Riko
reviews.text: Small portion, bad service, waiter said they give us more cuz some is small but they didn't.

reviews.author: Wen Zhang
reviews.text: Very delicious!

reviews.author: Kian Lam Koh
reviews.text: Love the food and reasonable prices

reviews: Lu Rou Fan (Braised Meat Rice) - you get a huge piece of braised meat, some braised minced pork, salted vegetables and a hardboiled egg. Read more: https://www.misstamchiak.com/a-wonder-bowl/
result.stall_name: A Wonder Bowl (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-101 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Harriot NN
reviews.text:
Quiet food center with many stalls manned by locals. Recommend these 2 stalls:

1) Fried carrot cake - carrot cakes appear to be made in house, saw trays of steamed carrot cakes. Well fried with loads of chay poh.

2) 满天香 Dumplings - Steamed dumplings and fried beancurb rolls come with generous fillings and are so delicious.

reviews.author: Moonlight Beauty
reviews.text:
🍜 Pork Ball Noodle Soup – Light, Healthy & Comforting!

There’s a small stall near my house that serves Pork Ball Noodle Soup, and ever since it opened, I’ve been going there quite often 😋

The soup is clear and naturally sweet, not oily at all.
The pork balls mixed with black fungus are chewy and flavorful — not greasy, so it’s perfect for anyone who prefers healthy, light meals.
The noodles are soft, with fresh bean sprouts and spring onions, making the bowl look and taste so clean and refreshing 🌿

It’s one of those dishes that’s simple yet satisfying, perfect for people who want to eat light but still get enough nutrition.
Pair it with an iced coffee and it’s the perfect lunch combo in Singapore ☕️✨

👉 If you live nearby, you should really try this place — the soup’s gentle flavor will surprise you in the best way!

reviews.author: Jude Lu
reviews.text: You kind find some gems here but don't expect it to blow your mind. 2 chefs is what people usually come here for. The food center is ok. The area is stuck in time and the has a really nostalgic vibe. Good for a short exploration and a quick fuss free meal.

reviews.author: Michi
reviews.text:
Tucked away in an unassuming corner of the hawker centre, I stumbled upon what might just be one of the best bowls of minced pork porridge I’ve had in a long time. It wasn’t planned, nor was I expecting much—just a quick meal to tide me through the day. But oh, was I in for a surprise.

Surprisingly one of the best minced pork porridges I’ve had! The uncle was super generous with the meat, and the porridge was smooth and comforting. Added an egg, total came to $5.50—not the cheapest, but absolutely worth it. I can’t stop thinking about it!

The hawker centre is also clean and dry. The cleaners are also very polite and friendly!

reviews.author: Grace T
reviews.text: This hawker center has great vibes on Saturdays. Uncles will be singing oldies while aunties at stalls close by will be cheering for them. What an amazing feeling of just people coming together in harmony. Of course some party pooper got to complain about them and the uncles were asked to stop singing. If they didn’t like the singing, they could sit on the other side of the hawker center because it’s not possible to hear them from the other side.  But no, this person chose to take away the happiness of many others. Now it’s just another hawker center. For food, charcoal bbq meat, bak Chor mee and chicken rice are the best!

reviews: Known for their fried fish ban mian. Fish was crispy and the noodles were chewy. Soup was also flavourful.
Ban Mian $3.50. Noodles were cooked just right. Fish was fresh and fried nicely.
Fried Fish Ban Mian - crispy fish and chewy noodles.
Tried the fried fish ban mian ($4) and it was pretty good. The fish remained crispy even after being in the soup for a while. The noodles were also quite chewy.
Ordered the dry ban mian with fried fish. The sauce was savoury and the fish was very crispy.
Good bowl of ban mian at a reasonable price.
result.stall_name: Hao Hao Noodle House (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.phone: This place does not have a landline.
contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-83 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Alex C
reviews.text: Al dente noodles and owner will toss it into the air to drain it if you order the dry version. Chilli is good and he is generous with the pork lard underneath the noodles. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Shocho Shocho
reviews.text:
I ordered Ipoh hor fun, and it was a delightful dish.
Generous amount of shredded chicken on top, with flavorful sauce poured on the noodles. It has strong taste that I like, unlike those less salt and umami weak ones that seems to be the trend now.
Side soup also got nice umami flavor, possibly from shitake mushrooms.
It doesn't have high stars on reviews, which I must disagree. I checked those who gave one star and saw what kind of food they prefer. Well, they post one star ones only. I don't trust posts by complainers. Glad that I trusted my eyes and checked this stall myself.

I will definitely come back again. Commonwealth got some nice stalls for sure.

reviews.author: 리ybbo비읍
reviews.text: Should have read the review before ordering. Noodles is normal, but oily. The sauce and chillis do not blend in at all. Char siew is dry. Wanton is mushy. Not my type of wanton noodles.

reviews.author: Hazel Nut
reviews.text: Springy noodles looks nicer than it taste. Wanton, noodle, and char siew are all below average. The only saving grace is generous vegetables and char siew.

reviews.author: MightyFoodie
reviews.text:
Old School Wanton Mee filled with Char Siew, Wanton with delicious braised of Chicken Feet. Overall is a nice plate of Wanton Mee but their Char Siew is too hard.
.
Location: Hao Hao Noodle House, 31 Commonwealth Cres, #02-83 Food Center, Singapore 149644

reviews: A variety of Ba Chor Mee / Fishball noodles options at this hardworking young hawker stall! Open till late!

I decided to try Bak Chor Mee ($2.70) out for lunch time while waiting for Burger at Hammee's. I saw long queue for this stall and most of people order Bak Chor Mee (dry). I haven't eaten Bak Chor Mee for a while because I am not really a fan of Bak Chor Mee. I didn't expected they put a lot of ingredients such as pig liver and minced meats inside Bak Chor Mee. It's worth it to try it out for portion size and price if you happen to be at the area.

A young hawker making delicious bak chor mee. The noodle is well mixed with sauces, vinegar and sambal chilli to create a flavourful bak chor mee. All ingredients are fresh and yummy too. Prices are very reasonable too. It ranges from $2.70 for small, $3.50 for medium and $4.50 for large. There’re also choices to add extra ingredients. Opens 7 days a week.
$2.70 for a bowl of bak chor mee its a steal! We opt for the large size for $4.50 instead. If you devour old school taste then you have to try this!

My bowl of Bak Chor Mee ($3.50) comes with a substantial amount of mee kia and a generous serving of ingredients such as slivers of mushroom, minced pork, pork slices, pork ball, pig's liver and not forgetting my added fish dumplings (4 pc for $1).
result.stall_name: Huang Da Fu (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.phone: This place does not have a landline.
contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-94 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

google.google_name: Huang Da Fu 黄大福 Bak Chor Mee

reviews.author: Sandra
reviews.text: Old school tasty Bak Chor Mee noodles done right. Vinegar and chilli in the perfect balance! We have been visiting this for the past few years. Our go to stall for Bak Chor Mee comfort! Owner is young but has the right expertise to churn out great noodles! The stall also sells you tiao which is one of the best I have tasted- super crispy! Stall owner was featured on CNA too.

reviews.author: Sze Wee Tan
reviews.text: Good Bak Chor Noodle, chilli and sauce are well balanced. Noodles nice bouncy

reviews.author: Stephen Chan
reviews.text: Kudos to Jimmy and crew for keeping hawker culture alive! Bak chor mee was good, guotiao cooked just right ( QQ like locals like). Seasoning spot on. $4.50 a bowl, value for money. Will try their fried fritters next time.

reviews.author: Sleepy Sleeping
reviews.text: The bak cho mee was nice. I like the noodle and chilli sauce. The noodles do not stick together even when i am finishing them unlike other mee pok. The owner was friendly too.

reviews.author: Terence Lee
reviews.text:
Come across this Bak chor mee stall, so decided to give it a try.
Noodles texture are great. Fresh ingredients and tasty soup. Chilli sauce is great. Not forgetting, the self help crispy pork lards.
Friendly young stall owner.
Did not regret trying, really good.
Recommend to try.

reviews: Premium Meats? ✅️ Cheap & Affordable? ✅️ Muslim-owned? ✅️ I’m not kidding when I say @salaibymeatdrop has something for everyone (unless you’re vegetarian oops)! With its super affordable price point, you’ll get to lepak with your friends and dig into a fantastic Smoke & Grill Meat Platter (SGD60) without breaking the bank. All 3 types of meat (Grilled Angus, Smoked Lamb Ribs & Full Grilled Chicken) were well-marinated and tender. Something unique to shout about would be their sides - besides the usual fries and salad, the platter also comes with smoked charcoal rice and grilled pita bread. A party on your tongue with each dip, their signature sauces (White Salai, Hickory BBQ, Garlic Chilli & Assam Salsa) are not to be missed! All in all, despite the slightly ulu location, I’d say @salaibymeatdrop is a great place to consider for your next hangout! For delivery orders, please WhatsApp them at 88745690! [Media Invite] 📍SALAI by MeatDrop Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre 31 Commonwealth Cres 02-71, S149644 🕦Closed on Mondays
result.stall_name: SALAI by MeatDrop (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-71 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Harriot NN
reviews.text:
Quiet food center with many stalls manned by locals. Recommend these 2 stalls:

1) Fried carrot cake - carrot cakes appear to be made in house, saw trays of steamed carrot cakes. Well fried with loads of chay poh.

2) 满天香 Dumplings - Steamed dumplings and fried beancurb rolls come with generous fillings and are so delicious.

reviews.author: Moonlight Beauty
reviews.text:
🍜 Pork Ball Noodle Soup – Light, Healthy & Comforting!

There’s a small stall near my house that serves Pork Ball Noodle Soup, and ever since it opened, I’ve been going there quite often 😋

The soup is clear and naturally sweet, not oily at all.
The pork balls mixed with black fungus are chewy and flavorful — not greasy, so it’s perfect for anyone who prefers healthy, light meals.
The noodles are soft, with fresh bean sprouts and spring onions, making the bowl look and taste so clean and refreshing 🌿

It’s one of those dishes that’s simple yet satisfying, perfect for people who want to eat light but still get enough nutrition.
Pair it with an iced coffee and it’s the perfect lunch combo in Singapore ☕️✨

👉 If you live nearby, you should really try this place — the soup’s gentle flavor will surprise you in the best way!

reviews.author: Jude Lu
reviews.text: You kind find some gems here but don't expect it to blow your mind. 2 chefs is what people usually come here for. The food center is ok. The area is stuck in time and the has a really nostalgic vibe. Good for a short exploration and a quick fuss free meal.

reviews.author: Michi
reviews.text:
Tucked away in an unassuming corner of the hawker centre, I stumbled upon what might just be one of the best bowls of minced pork porridge I’ve had in a long time. It wasn’t planned, nor was I expecting much—just a quick meal to tide me through the day. But oh, was I in for a surprise.

Surprisingly one of the best minced pork porridges I’ve had! The uncle was super generous with the meat, and the porridge was smooth and comforting. Added an egg, total came to $5.50—not the cheapest, but absolutely worth it. I can’t stop thinking about it!

The hawker centre is also clean and dry. The cleaners are also very polite and friendly!

reviews.author: Grace T
reviews.text: This hawker center has great vibes on Saturdays. Uncles will be singing oldies while aunties at stalls close by will be cheering for them. What an amazing feeling of just people coming together in harmony. Of course some party pooper got to complain about them and the uncles were asked to stop singing. If they didn’t like the singing, they could sit on the other side of the hawker center because it’s not possible to hear them from the other side.  But no, this person chose to take away the happiness of many others. Now it’s just another hawker center. For food, charcoal bbq meat, bak Chor mee and chicken rice are the best!

reviews: Jian Kang Noodles serves up a pretty decent old school wanton mee. Springy el dente noodles, wanton was pretty decent as well. And the chilli was quite solid.
Old fashioned wanton mee with soikao (水饺) added as a side for a sweetheart deal
Dumplings soup (5 pcs - $3) 😍😋👍🏼 .

reviews.author: Ryan T
reviews.text: Delicious old school wanton noodle. Good blend of sauce and chili, QQ noodle and yummy wantons. Pork rib is succulent and tender.

reviews.author: Stephen Chan
reviews.text:
Timeless classic! Been serving wanton noodles for ages, and for good reason.

HK-style thin noodles (yiu mian) are crunchy and seasoned to perfection - delicious!

Wanton's also nicely done.

Char siew could be better, but doesn't detract from the overall goodness.

Still a top spot for wanton noodle fans.

reviews.author: Singapore人
reviews.text: Good portion of noodle, meat, and vegetables. Not the typical thin wanton noodles but still good. Had the spicy version and the chilli sauce was great.

reviews.author: Tengcc Ng
reviews.text: The charsiew wanton noodle texture & taste is very good, condiments is just right which makes the overall makan experience very pleasant. Ho Jiak!!!!

reviews.author: Taste & See
reviews.text: Pretty delicious wanton mee. But the taste and texture of noodles and wantons were not quite my type. Soup tastes a little on the sweet side. Kailan veges were fresh and nicely boiled. I added wantons for $1 more. $5.50

reviews: My braised duck rice ($3) comes with a good portion of duck meat and some cucumber slices atop a plate of white rice. A sprig of cilantro goes on top and a liberal dressing of dark soy was drizzled all over. No soup was given. . . Due to the dark sauce drenched over the duck meat, I could not taste the flavor of the lor it was braised in. The meat is basically flavorless to me. . . Fortunately, the saving grace is their super tangy chicken rice chili which I poured into the rice and the tangy flavor makes everything easier to swallow. . . google.google_name: Chinatown Ah Po Braised Duck 牛车水阿婆卤鸭

reviews.author: SG Food Legends
reviews.text:
Hawker center not much crowd which I like.

I chanced upon this after eating my favourite char siew rice in the same hawker center.

I dabao home this braised duck and many ingredients. It is still so tasty after I bought it home to eat!

This stall is a gem and the boss is nice! Oh yes expect some queue!

reviews.author: King
reviews.text:
Food is excellent, service is good.

The service is slow, because the owner try to give u a perfect food. She is slow and proud of her work. The 3 special sauce are really good. The whole process of preparation is like at home your mother prepared for u.

Must try !!!

reviews.author: J T
reviews.text:
Great food, nice bosses. Went a couple of time for lunch. Small queue.

Each plate is plated nicely. Especially love the chilli. Very 'open taste'. This hawker seem to have a few nice stalls judging by the q but I ended up eating this a few times. Cuz nice food la. Lol

reviews.author: WT Wong
reviews.text:
Had an excellent braised duck meal — the duck was soft and succulent, with just the right bite. The sauce was fragrant and balanced; slightly salty but not overpowering, letting the natural flavors come through. Each piece was neatly cut to a good thickness and size for easy eating.

The pig ears stood out — tender yet chewy, done just right. The braised egg was flavorful all the way through, showing it had been properly steeped. The porridge was smooth and comforting, subtly seasoned and paired well with the other dishes.

Altogether, a very satisfying and well-executed meal.

reviews.author: Ryan T
reviews.text: 7/10. Tasty duck rice. The one I ate had a few small bones, otherwise very good. Sauce is their own special blend.

reviews: Open only from Thur to Sun, I will only pick this period to come to this market as I must have a bowl of “Tong Sui” from Xi Le Ting 喜乐亭 before I leave. Each bowl only $1.00. So good. Location: Xi Le Ting 喜乐亭, Commonwealth Crescent Market and Food Centre, 31 Commonwealth Cres, #02-70, Singapore 149644

I will always order this whenever I am here for a meal. Its actually so good that I will tends to order another bowl. 😅 . They only serve 4 types of hot dessert, Green Bean, Red Bean, Cheng Teng and Mak chuk (Sweet Wheat Porridge), all at $1.00 per bowl. . Location: Xi Le Ting 喜乐亭, Commonwealth Crescent Market and Food Centre, No. 70, Singapore 149644

Xi Le Ting serves up old-school desserts that have recipes that are being perfected since half a century ago. You would spot an auntie behind the stall scooping endlessly, but do not expect her to smile much. Recommendation: please be precise and clear with orders, and don't be surprised if some desserts are sold out early, early lunch. There are four options at the stall, namely the famous Cheng Tng ($1), Green Bean Soup ($1), Red Bean Soup ($1) and Sweet Wheat Porridge ($1). Takeaways are priced at $1.20. The refreshing Cheng Tng contains plentiful of ingredients from white fungus, dried longan, barley, dried persimmon to 'pang da hai' (boat sterculia seed). Love that this is very nostalgic-tasting served in a traditional porcelain bowl and is super affordable.

All I want now is this bowl of hot traditional Cheng Tng to drive away my Monday blues. The old (and known to be grumpy) granny at the stall insists on cooking the 4 items on her menu using the same recipes for nearly half a century till date. Especially delighted by the slice of dried persimmon, that adds a hint of sweetness, in addition to the usual suspects of dried longan, white fungus, barley, large sago. And perhaps you might have always wondered what's that black, seaweed-looking thing floating atop the soup. It's the Malva Nut, something that I enjoy for its texture. Served in a traditional porcelain bowl truly brings about a nostalgic taste, as I got reminded of the same bowl used when dining at my late grandfather's house. Xi Le Ting also offers green bean soup (which seems to be the most popular choice!), red bean soup and sweet wheat porridge.

A great find at Commonwealth Crescent Market, a small bowl of happiness, these traditional Chinese desserts are tended by an old granny, and they don't cost much! :') Back to the good old days (like for me, the 90's rocks) when things are all cheap and good!

I probably would just call it 大麥 because that's the name I grew up knowing. Quite a surprising find at Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre — an increasingly hard-to-find local dessert these days. From Xi Le Ting 喜樂亭, on first look it does seem a tad watered down, though I thought the sweetness was actually just right without being too bland. The wheat pearls come with a nice chewy bite, almost akin to having sago, though I would really wish for some coconut milk for a silkier and aromatic feel and taste. Still, it's pretty much a rare find, and this is one that I find made pretty well especially with its rarity these days. Very affordable at $0.80 for a bowl too!
result.stall_name: Xi Le Ting (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.phone: This place does not have a landline.
contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-70 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: V
reviews.text:
14 July 2023 (Friday): Arrived at 10.45am and there was no queue. Tried the green bean soup and wheat porridge, $1 each (cash only), both are very good! Green bean soup has sago and coconut milk.

30 June 2023 (Friday): Came all the way to try this today at 10+ am and it was closed.

reviews.author: CK C
reviews.text: Rare dessert stall that still retains the traditional way of doing things (in a good way eg. using proper ceramic bowls and not disposable plastics). Auntie offers four types of warm dessert - cheng tng, green bean, red bean and da mai. Each bowl costs only $1 ($1.20 for take away), which is exceptionally good for its price.

reviews.author: MightyFoodie
reviews.text:
I will always order this whenever I am here for a meal. Its actually so good that I will tends to order another bowl. 😅
.
They only serve 4 types of hot dessert, Green Bean, Red Bean, Cheng Teng and Mak chuk (Sweet Wheat Porridge), all at $1.00 per bowl.
.
Location: Xi Le Ting 喜乐亭, Commonwealth Crescent Market and Food Centre, Stall No. 70, Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Euphorie Liew
reviews.text:
You will definitely see a long queue forming at this stall. And it's always nice to have a bowl of dessert after lunch.

Ah Mah only sells 4 types of traditional desserts - Green Bean Soup, Red Bean Soup, Cheng Teng and Da Mai. All selling at $1 each while takeaway will be $1.20.

The only thing is that they only serve it HOT, no cold version! Besides that, you can request for less sweet or less syrup.

Their Red Beans 🫘 look like 赤豆 to me 粒粒分明, not those mushy type. Cheng Teng has the dried persimmon in it, whereby you hardly find them anymore in other stall. Sweetness is just nice.

And the dessert were served in ceramic bowls, so it may be heavy when you ordering a few bowls. To highlight, their trays are those round plastic tray, they are thinner in material and tray may not be able to hold on to many of the bowls.

reviews.author: lynn
reviews.text:
100% value for money! It’s only $1 per bowl, yet it comes with a generous amount of ingredients.

You’re also able to ask for less syrup so that it’s healthier too :) I got mine less sweet, and it was still full of flavour, really enjoyed it!

The wheat soup (大麦） is most popular, so head down early if you want to try that. I visited at 1pm and it was sold out alr :(

reviews: The crackling pork belly skin is irresistible, while the savoury char siu is tender and full of flavour. Not bad indeed!
The duck skin is crispy, while the duck meat is tender, good balance and flavour. Indeed satisfying!

Foong Kee was returned, and this time at Commonwealth Crescent Market and Food Centre. Having eaten at their Keong Saik Outlet before its closure back in 2021. Already expect good food here, and was not disappointed. My other half having this for the first time agreed that the Sio Bak was very good. Sio Bak excellent. Crispy skin, tender meat. Char siew with its charred edges was very good too. Meat had a good meat and fat ratio. Come earlier if u want roasted duck. By the time i came at 12.30pm, no more roasted duck liao

Foong kee is back!! Beautifully roasted meats as usual. The queue speaks volumes. The charsiew is nicely caramelised, tender yet not too fatty. Roast pork has a crisp skin, meat is surprisingly juicy given that it's quite lean, and isn't too salty. Rice is average, but paired with a savoury sweet sauce, and refreshing sliced cucumbers. Come before 12.30, because you'll have to queue for a bit and they sold out by the time we left at about 1.30pm on a Sat.

Their wanton mee is v nice! It's thin & very springy, coated in a light sauce (I think it might be sesame oil + their charsiew sauce?). V similar to feifei roasted noodles @ yuhua (my fav!). But I did feel like there was a slight alkaline taste. Wantons had quite a decent amount of meat filling, liked that they used water chestnuts so there was the cronch element. The charsiew is really good - it's tender and juicy, there's a nice mix of fatty + lean parts, and the ends have a nice slight char so those parts are crisp. It is feels “cleaner” and not as sinful compared to other charsiews out there, but still v delicious so I liked it a lot! They're closing on 15 June because the owner wants to take a rest! We actually queued almost 3 hours for this on sat. It's a v nice satisfying meal, glad we tried it before it closed, since it's been on my wishlist since 2 years ago hahaha

Got a long time never eat roast food such as char siew and also roasted meat ($5) at outside because I always taobao back home for neighbourhood coffee shop. Didn't expected that char siew got that strong charocal taste but I felt that its quite fattening for me. Probably need to request for lean meat. I also like their roasted meat not that salty and also fattening too. 😅
result.stall_name: Foong Kee Traditional Charcoal Roaster (Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre)

contact_and_links.address: 31 Commonwealth Crescent #02-90 Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre Singapore 149644

reviews.author: Ankori
reviews.text:
I decided to give this roast stall a try, but honestly, it was a disappointing experience. They state that they open at 11:00, yet they only started serving at 11:15, leaving a long queue of customers waiting. To make matters worse, while everyone was standing in line, a customer sitting at a nearby table , clearly someone they knew was allowed to place and collect his takeaway order before the stall even officially opened. This kind of double standard shows they prioritize regulars over everyone else, which doesn’t leave a good impression.

As for the food, it doesn’t live up to the hype. The roast duck is tender but lacks seasoning , it tastes more like plain meat than something well-marinated, even if the skin is somewhat crispy. The roast pork was underwhelming: not crispy, with certain parts tasting a little too porky and lacking in salt. The char siew is the only saving grace, with a nice char and sweetness, but it leans heavily on fatty cuts, which not everyone will enjoy.

Overall, I don’t think it’s worth queuing up and putting up with the stall’s attitude. There are plenty of other roast stalls around that serve equal or better quality food without making customers feel like second-class diners.

reviews.author: Freddy Kua
reviews.text: This stall serves delicous roasted meat at hawker price. No wonder there was a queue at lunch time. Love their caramelised char siew, served in generous chunks, with just rhe right amount of fat. The roasted portion belly was delicious too. With crispy skin and lots of lean portion, the way I prefer. Their roasted duck can hold its ground too, when compared to other popular stalls. Having a platter of mix roated meat would be a great way to enjoy what this stall has to offer.

reviews.author: SG Food Legends
reviews.text: Queued before is opened and taste is as good as ever! Too bad no dumpling soup anymore. Love this place.

reviews.author: C K Leong (Ah Leong San)
reviews.text:
Since the owners decided to reopen here, I haven’t been here. Standard is still good. I ordered the 3 in 1 - roast duck, roast pork and char siew rice. All three items are still good. Charcoal roasted is always better. Roasted duck and pork skins are crispy and I love the char siew not too sweet ❤️ Stall opens every Tuesdays to Saturdays from 11 am till sold out.

Second visit to try roasted duck drumstick and roasted pork. Yummy! Pity I forgot to ask for sauce on the duck. Would have made it better.

reviews.author: Darren Tan
reviews.text: The char siew is a must to try.. but do expect a wait about 20 mins but I think it’s worth the wait .. roast pork was normal.
